current_web_presence: No website — only Google Maps listing and word of mouth
why_they_need_website: 759 reviews showing massive demand. No online menu, no delivery ordering, no branding. Losing customers to competitors with proper web presence.
notes: Verified 759 reviews on Google Maps. No website button visible in Google profile. Heavy F&B traffic sector — customers check menu online before visiting.

current_web_presence: No website — Facebook page exists but no dedicated site
why_they_need_website: 322 reviews with no home page. Heritage brand needs digital preservation. Tourists and locals search for Hainanese coffee online.
notes: 322 Google reviews. Cultural/heritage F&B — strong draw for tourists and foodies. No website despite being a landmark in Seremban.

current_web_presence: Zero digital footprint — no website, no social media, no delivery platforms
why_they_need_website: Zero online presence despite 250+ reviews. Hawker-to-web pipeline can turn a local gem into a destination. Online ordering alone would capture untapped revenue.
notes: Approx 250 reviews. Complete digital void — no website, no Facebook, no delivery. Classic case of a quality product invisible online.

current_web_presence: Foodpanda only — no own website or direct ordering channel
why_they_need_website: Relies entirely on Foodpanda for digital orders (30% commission). Own website with direct ordering = higher margins and customer ownership.
notes: ~200 reviews. Trapped on Foodpanda. Serious need to escape third-party commissions with a direct ordering website.

current_web_presence: No website — no online presence beyond Google Maps
why_they_need_website: 481 reviews = huge loyal base. No website means no reservation system, no event booking, no menu showcase. Losing corporate event bookings.
notes: 481 Google reviews. Chinese seafood — a high-ticket F&B category perfect for event/corporate bookings through a website.

current_web_presence: Facebook + delivery platforms only — no proper website
why_they_need_website: 500+ reviews with 4.8 stars = Seremban legend. No website means no brand hub, no catering inquiries, no online merchandise. Brand dilution.
notes: 500+ reviews, 4.8 stars. Iconic kopitiam with massive brand potential. Website could drive catering, merchandise, and franchise interest.

current_web_presence: Facebook only — no e-commerce or online catalog
why_they_need_website: Traditional bakery with 150+ reviews. Perfect for e-commerce — cookies, biscuits, gift boxes shipped nationwide. No online store = leaving money on table.
notes: ~150 reviews. Heritage bakery with huge gifting/e-commerce potential. No online ordering despite being a beloved local brand.

current_web_presence: Phone-only — no website, no online booking, no social media presence
why_they_need_website: 180 reviews with phone-only contact. Car detailing customers search online for pricing, portfolio (before/after photos), and booking. No website = invisible to new car owners.
notes: ~180 reviews. Auto detailing — high-ticket service. Customers need to see portfolio photos and pricing before booking. No website = lost leads.

current_web_presence: Booking platforms only — Fresha/Booksy but no own-brand website
why_they_need_website: 254 reviews at PERFECT 5.0 — peak reputation. Relies on third-party booking platforms (paying commissions). Own site with direct booking = brand control + higher margin.
notes: 254 reviews, 5.0 stars. Premium barbershop trapped on booking aggregators. Perfect candidate for a JRV Systems booking website.

current_web_presence: Social media only — Instagram/Facebook but no e-commerce website
why_they_need_website: 170 reviews at 5.0 stars. Pet owners actively search for grooming booking and pet product e-commerce. No website = zero online revenue from a booming pet economy.
notes: 170+ reviews, 5.0 stars. Pet services sector is booming in Malaysia. E-commerce for pet products + online grooming booking would unlock significant revenue.
